summary: dotenv-express ships verbatim dotenv source under a confusable name
details: The package is published as `dotenv-express` but its contents are the legitimate `motdotla/dotenv` package verbatim: `package.json` declares `repository: git://github.com/motdotla/dotenv.git`, the README is titled `# dotenv`, and `lib/main.js` is dotenv's own source (parse.env, populate process.env, optional vault decrypt). There is no Express-specific code, no lifecycle scripts (`preinstall`/`install`/`postinstall`/`prepare`), no network I/O, no credential reads beyond the documented `DOTENV_CONFIG_*` envs, and no obfuscation. The concern is name confusion: a developer reaching for `dotenv` may install `dotenv-express` instead, taking on a separately maintained republish whose future versions are not controlled by the upstream `dotenv` maintainers. Routing to human review so a maintainer can decide whether the name-confusion warrants an advisory; the current version contains no installer-harm payload.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
